comments.body:
No actionable comments were generated in the recent review. 🎉
ℹ️ Recent review info
⚙️ Run configuration **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro Plus **Run ID**: `6d43f83f-743c-4089-8361-39b75f527faa`
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 09b3750ac3766d066e869a9b7ff37b4a6028f6d5 and 4e5326aba502020faa259b2f6b34f1986e4c72e9.
📒 Files selected for processing (1) * `particle-scene.js`
✅ Files skipped from review due to trivial changes (1) * particle-scene.js
---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Particle colors and background now derive from the current theme brightness. A single fixed palette is replaced by `DARK_PALETTE` and `LIGHT_PALETTE` with runtime selection. Scene background and particle colors are initialized from the active palette, and an `updateColors()` method enables dynamic synchronization when the theme changes. ## Changes **Theme-aware particle colors** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Palette definitions and theme helpers**
`particle-scene.js` | Single `PALETTE` replaced by `DARK_PALETTE` and `LIGHT_PALETTE`. Added `getThemeBg()`, `isLightBg()`, and `activePalette()` to read `--c-bg`, compute brightness, and select the palette at runtime. | | **Scene background initialization**
`particle-scene.js` | Scene background is set from computed theme background via `getThemeBg()` instead of hard-coded color. | | **Particle color initialization**
`particle-scene.js` | Particle vertex colors assigned from the active theme palette during creation instead of fixed palette. | | **Dynamic color updates**
`particle-scene.js` | `updateColors()` method recomputes `scene.background` and reassigns all particle RGB values from the active palette, marking the color buffer attribute for GPU update. | ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~12 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 I hopped through palettes, light and deep, > I sampled hues while others sleep. > From CSS bg the colors stream, > Into the buffer, bright they gleam. > A rabbit cheers the theme's new dream.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 0.00% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title directly and accurately describes the main change: making the particle scene background and color palette responsive to the current theme rather than using hardcoded values. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `fix/particle-scene-theme-aware-bg`

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@particle-scene.js`:
- Around line 52-61: The isLightBg function only parses hex colors; update it to
accept and detect rgb()/rgba() and hsl()/hsla() CSS formats (in addition to
existing 3/6-char hex) by: 1) trimming the input and checking patterns for /^#/,
/^rgb/, and /^hsl/; 2) for rgb/rgba parse the numeric R,G,B components (ignore
alpha), for hsl/hsla convert H,S,L to RGB using a standard HSL→RGB helper, and
for hex keep existing logic; 3) then compute perceived brightness the same way
and return the boolean. Update or add helper functions named like parseRgbString
and hslToRgb and use isLightBg to accept the --c-bg value in any of those
formats.
```

</details>
